content:
A few months ago, I would’ve laughed if someone told me you could make real money from a website getting only 100 visitors a day. That’s not a typo. Not 100,000. Not 10,000. A hundred.
Most “make money online” articles love to flash screenshots of six-figure traffic and five-figure earnings, which is great if you’re already there — but completely useless if you’re just starting out with a tiny, brand-new site that barely gets noticed by Google yet.
So this article isn’t about chasing massive traffic. It’s about what actually works when your numbers are small, your site is young, and you just want your website to start paying for itself instead of sitting there as a hobby that costs you hosting fees every month.
The Mistake I Made First
When I first tried to monetize my site, I did what everyone tells you to do: apply to Google AdSense.
Big mistake — or at least, premature.
With only around 100 visitors a day, AdSense earnings were embarrassing. We’re talking cents, not dollars, on most days. AdSense is built around scale. It rewards sites that already have thousands of daily visitors and strong organic search rankings. A small, young site like mine barely registered.
I don’t say this to discourage you. I say it because I wasted weeks assuming AdSense was “the” way to monetize, when really, it was just one option that happened to be a bad fit for where I was.
What Actually Changed Things For Me
The real shift happened when I stopped thinking about monetization as “put ads on the site and hope” and started thinking about which ad network was actually designed to work well with small, low-traffic sites — not just tolerate them.
That’s when I found Monetag.
Unlike a lot of the bigger networks, Monetag doesn’t require a huge traffic threshold before you’re accepted or before you start seeing meaningful payouts. The approval process was quick, I wasn’t stuck in a review queue for weeks, and I started seeing actual earnings show up almost immediately after I added their ad units.
Why Monetag Works So Well for Small Sites
Here’s what made the real difference, in plain terms:
1. It doesn’t punish you for having low traffic. A lot of ad networks essentially treat small publishers as an afterthought. Monetag’s model is built to work with publishers at every stage, so 100 visitors a day isn’t treated as “too small to matter.”
2. Multiple ad formats mean more chances to earn from the same visitors. Instead of relying on a single banner ad type, Monetag offers formats like push notifications, in-page push, interstitials, and native ads. This matters a lot when your traffic is small — you want to squeeze meaningful value out of every visitor without needing 10x the traffic to see 10x the ad impressions.
3. Low payout threshold. When you’re only getting a small trickle of earnings each day, waiting to hit a $100 payout minimum can feel like forever. Monetag’s threshold is far more realistic for someone just starting out, so you can actually see and withdraw your money instead of watching a balance sit untouched for months.
4. It’s not limited to just website traffic. This was honestly the biggest unlock for me. Monetag doesn’t require your traffic to come strictly from a website. You can monetize traffic from social media platforms, Telegram channels, link-in-bio pages, and even URL shorteners. So while my website was building up its 100 daily visitors organically, I was also running a small Telegram channel and pushing some traffic through social posts — and Monetag let me monetize all of that too, not just the website.
That combination — low barrier to entry, multiple ad formats, and support for non-website traffic — is exactly what a small publisher needs before their traffic scales up.
What My Actual Setup Looked Like
To be transparent, here’s roughly how I approached it with such small numbers:

• I placed a mix of in-page push and native ad units on my site, avoiding overly aggressive formats that could annoy the handful of visitors I did have. With low traffic, every visitor matters, and a bad experience means they don’t come back.

• I linked a Telegram channel where I shared updates related to my niche, and ran Monetag’s link monetization on outbound clicks from that channel.

• I kept things simple. I didn’t try to stack five different ad networks on a 100-visitor site. That just creates clutter and a worse user experience for very little extra earning potential at that traffic level.
The earnings weren’t life-changing in the first month, and I want to be honest about that. Nobody making 100 visitors a day is going to quit their job overnight. But it was the first time my website earnings felt proportional to my actual traffic instead of feeling like I was shouting into the void for nothing.
CPM Expectations at Low Traffic (Be Realistic)
If you’re at 100 visitors a day, don’t expect the same CPM numbers you see quoted by big affiliate marketers with six-figure traffic. CPMs depend heavily on your niche, visitor country, device type, and ad format — and they naturally fluctuate. What matters more at this stage isn’t hitting some ideal CPM number; it’s making sure the network you’re using doesn’t ignore you just because your numbers are small.
That’s really the core lesson here: at low traffic, the “best” ad network isn’t the one with the flashiest advertised CPM. It’s the one that actually works with the traffic level you’re at right now.
Why I Choose Monetag
If I had to boil it down to one sentence: Monetag was the first network that treated my small site like it was worth monetizing, instead of making me feel like I needed 10,000 daily visitors just to be taken seriously.
Combine that with:

• Fast approval

• A payout threshold that doesn’t feel impossibly far away

• Support for social media, Telegram, and link traffic — not just websites

• Multiple ad formats to test what actually performs for your specific audience
…and it became the obvious choice for where I currently am in my website’s growth. As my traffic grows, I may add other networks into the mix, but Monetag is staying as my foundation.
If you want to see what it looks like for your own site, here’s where I signed up:
monetag.com
Final Thoughts
If you’re sitting on a small website with barely any traffic and assuming monetization isn’t worth attempting yet, I’d push back on that. You don’t need six-figure traffic to start earning something real. You need a network that’s actually built to work with where you are today, not where you hope to be in a year.
Start small, keep your ad placements clean so you don’t scare off the visitors you do have, and don’t be afraid to monetize traffic outside your website too. A hundred visitors a day isn’t nothing — it’s a starting point, and with the right network behind it, it can actually start paying you back.
